intro:
I couldn't find any information on Coriandrum tordylium, but I believe you may be referring to Coriandrum sativum. Here is a paragraph about Coriandrum sativum:

Coriandrum sativum, commonly known as coriander, is a plant in the parsley family native to the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of coriander include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, and it has been traditionally used to aid digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of coriander include limonene, linalool, and pinene, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coriander is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as in traditional dishes and spice blends.
